attributes.description: Alerts when `sqlserver.deadlock.rate > 0`. Sustained non-zero deadlock rate is a serious concurrency problem.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Deadlocks detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server deadlocks detected

### What fired
`sqlserver.deadlock.rate > 0` during the window.

### Why it matters
Deadlocks abort one or more transactions to break a cycle. Sustained deadlocks point to inconsistent locking order or overly broad transactions.

### Triage
1. Examine deadlock XML graphs from Extended Events.
2. Look for hot tables / indexes.

### Remediation
- Enforce consistent access order.
- Use appropriate isolation levels and smaller transactions.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero rate.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Any non-zero sustained deadlock rate is a serious concurrency problem
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.deadlock.rate IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_deadlocks = MAX(metrics.sqlserver.deadlock.rate),
       instance_id = MAX(resource.attributes.service.instance.id)
  BY COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown")
| WHERE max_deadlocks > 0
| KEEP instance_id, max_deadlocks
